1. The expected value of $ \Delta y_{t+1} $ given $ \Delta y_t = 0.02 $ and $ \Delta y_{t-1} = 0.01 $, assuming $ \epsilon_{t+1} = 0 $, can be calculated using the given ARIMA model:

$$
\Delta y_{t+1} = 0.5 + 0.6 \Delta y_t - 0.3 \Delta y_{t-1} + \epsilon_{t+1} + 0.4 \epsilon_{t}
$$

Substituting the given values:

$$
\Delta y_{t+1} = 0.5 + 0.6 \times 0.02 - 0.3 \times 0.01 + 0 + 0.4 \times 0
$$

$$
\Delta y_{t+1} = 0.5 + 0.012 - 0.003 + 0 = 0.509
$$

Thus, the expected value of $ \Delta y_{t+1} $ is $ 0.509 $.

2. Using the regression model:

$$
\Delta y_t = \beta_0 + \beta_1 S_t + u_t
$$

Given $ \beta_0 = 0.01 $, $ \beta_1 = 0.03 $, and $ S_t = 0.5 $:

$$
\Delta y_t = 0.01 + 0.03 \times 0.5 + u_t = 0.01 + 0.015 + u_t = 0.025 + u_t
$$

Assuming $ u_t $ is negligible, the predicted GDP growth rate is $ 0.025 $.

P.S.: The model combines time series analysis and machine learning to improve forecasting accuracy. The results from both sub-problems are used to optimize the forecasting model.
